dsHPC: DataSHIELD Interface for High-Performance Computing

Source: R/dsHPC-package.R
dsHPC-package.Rd

A server-side package providing DataSHIELD with access to High-Performance Computing capabilities.

Details

The dsHPC package enables DataSHIELD users to submit computationally intensive functions to HPC clusters, monitor job status, and retrieve results efficiently. The package provides an interface to job schedulers (currently Slurm) and includes a caching system to avoid redundant computations.

Key features include:

  • Submit R functions to be executed on HPC clusters

  • Job status monitoring and management

  • Efficient caching of results based on function+arguments hashing

  • DataSHIELD-specific interfaces for secure server-side operations

  • Function wrapping utilities to simplify HPC interactions

Basic Workflow

  1. Initialize the system with dsHPC.init()

  2. Submit a job with dsHPC.submit()

  3. Check job status with dsHPC.status()

  4. Retrieve results with dsHPC.result()

DataSHIELD Integration

For DataSHIELD environments, use the dsHPC.ds.* functions, which provide DataSHIELD-compatible interfaces to the core functionality.

Author

Maintainer: David Sarrat González david.sarrat@isglobal.org

Authors:

  • Xavier Escribà-Montagut xavier.escriba@isglobal.org

  • Juan R González juanr.gonzalez@isglobal.org

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Initialize the system
dsHPC.init()

# Submit a job
job <- dsHPC.submit(
  func_name = "kmeans",
  args = list(x = my_data, centers = 3),
  package = "stats"
)

# Check job status
status <- dsHPC.status(job$job_id)

# Get result (with waiting)
result <- dsHPC.result(job$job_id, wait_for_completion = TRUE)
} # }
